text:
I'm Home × Welcome Back × I'm Killua (ただいま×おかえり×ぼくキルア, Tadaima × Okaeri × Boku Kirua) is the 46 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It first aired on November 18 th , 2000.

Overview

Gon and Killua go to Whale Island, where Gon grew up. Mito welcomes them and asks about the exam. When camping, they hear a shot and see poachers attacking a Foxbear and its cub. Easily beating them, they take the cub home. Gon wants to save him, but Killua insists they stop his suffering by killing him. Finally, they heal him with their Nen.

Synopsis

Killua and Gon take a ship to Whale Island. On the way there, Killua struggles with memories of his older brother while trying to think about his future goals. When they arrive on Whale Island, Killua is shocked to learn that they must walk to Gon's house, which Gon estimates will take just under a day.

As they approach Gon's house, Mito is outside hanging up the laundry and is ecstatic to see Gon coming up the trail. Gon runs ahead to see her, and Killua thinks about how weird and rural the area is. When Killua catches up to Gon, Gon's grandmother also comes out to greet them. Mito chastises Gon for worrying her so much since Gon forgot to write to her after the Hunter Exam finished. Gon introduces Killua to them; Mito mentions that Killua is the first friend Gon has ever brought home.

Inside Gon's house, the four of them eat together as a family and talk afterward. Gon tells Mito about the Hunter Exam and shows her his new Hunter License. Gon and Killua then go out to the forest together; Gon takes Killua out to several scenic spots and regales Killua with a few of his stories about Konta.

The following day, Konta notices some suspicious men in the forest. Mito brings flowers to a grave, likely belonging to Gon's mother, while the boys stay home with Gon's grandmother to watch the shop. Killua falls asleep on the job, and when he awakes, Mito is back and giving Gon a haircut outside. Killua also gets a haircut and thinks about how nice things are going while Mito cuts his hair.

The boys go camping in the forest that night, and Killua asks Gon about his plans for the near future. Gon plans to stay on Whale Island to try and find out about his dad until he needs to be in Yorknew City in September. Killua vents to Gon about not knowing what he wants to do with his life, and Gon offers to travel the world with Killua so that Killua can search out what he wants to do while Gon looks for his father. Killua is excited about the offer and happily accepts. 

When Killua asks about Gon's mother, Gon can provide very little information about her, claiming Mito to be the only person he thinks of as a mom. The boys talk about how nice Mito is, and Mito, who had been listening to their conversation from behind a tree, walks off.

Later, the boys are interrupted by a gunshot, and they run toward the sound as Gon explains that it must be poachers. When they find the poachers, Konta is in chains, and his cub is injured. Before one of the poachers can shoot Konta, Gon and Killua disarm him and scare the rest of the poachers off. They try to get the chains off of Konta, but Konta gets them to shift their attention to his cub, who is dying. The boys rush back home with the cub in Gon's arms.

Back at the house, the boys fight over what to do about the cub. Killua insists that he should kill the cub because of how much pain it is in and how it won't recover, but Gon insists that he can save the cub and won't allow Killua to get close to it. Mito hears the boys fighting and tries to stop them, but Mito and Gon get thrown to the ground in the process, and Mito's arm gets injured. Killua is about to kill the cub, acting like he's stuck in a trance when Mito slaps him. She lectures him on the importance of trying to save a life, and Gon convinces Killua that they might be able to heal the cub using Nen.

Together, the boys manage to heal the cub, and the next day they bring him back to Konta, who appears grateful. Killua apologizes to Mito, who now has bandages all up her forearm. She tells Killua not to worry about it, brings both boys in for a hug, and tells them how proud she is of them. Killua is stunned before settling into the hug.

Later, Konta is seen sitting with his cub and is shocked to see him exuding a strong, glowing aura.
